channel.title: NYT > Top Stories
channel.copyright: Copyright 2026 The New York Times Company

item.title: Trump and Rubio Insist Iran War Is Over, Even as Missiles Fly During Cease-Fire
item.description: The White House is turning to rhetorical leaps as President Trump tries to put the biggest political crisis of his presidency behind him.
item.dc:creator: David E. Sanger
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
United States Defense and Military Forces
item.media:credit: Tierney L. Cross/The New York Times
item.media:description: Despite Secretary of State Marco Rubio’s declaration that the objectives of the war have been accomplished, they clearly have not.

item.title: Trump Says U.S. Pausing Efforts to Guide Ships Through Strait Blocked by Iran
item.description: President Trump said a U.S. blockade of the Strait of Hormuz would “remain in full force.” The shift came after the defense secretary said U.S. efforts to free trapped vessels would continue.
item.dc:creator: Erica L. Green, Eric Schmitt, Greg Jaffe and Leo Sands
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
United States Defense and Military Forces
item.media:credit: Arash Khamooshi for The New York Times
item.media:description: A billboard in Tehran showing the three supreme leaders of Iran last week, only one of whom is alive.

item.title: In Indiana Primary Elections, Most Trump-Backed Challengers Beat Incumbents
item.description: President Trump endorsed primary opponents against seven Republican lawmakers who opposed his redistricting push. At least five of the Trump-backed candidates have won their races.
item.dc:creator: Mitch Smith
item.media:credit: Cara Penquite/Associated Press
item.media:description: Voters at the polls in West Lafayette, Ind., on Tuesday.

item.title: Takeaways From Indiana Primary Elections 2026: Trump Gets Payback
item.description: The president helped unseat most of the state lawmakers he targeted after they rebuffed his call to draw new House maps to help Republicans.
item.dc:creator: Mitch Smith and Reid J. Epstein
item.media:credit: Maddie McGarvey for The New York Times
item.media:description: The Indiana Statehouse in Indianapolis.

item.title: 5 Takeaways From the California Gubernatorial Debate
item.description: Seven candidates sparred in the first nationally televised debate in the 2026 California governor’s race. Many tried to launch attacks in a last-ditch effort to gain ground on their opponents.
item.dc:creator: Laurel Rosenhall and Jill Cowan
item.media:credit: Gabriela Bhaskar/The New York Times
item.media:description: Seven candidates engaged in a spirited debate hosted by CNN at East Los Angeles College on Tuesday.

item.title: G.O.P. Proposes $1 Billion in Immigration Bill for Trump’s Ballroom Project
item.description: The money would go toward security improvements as part of an East Wing construction project, including a new ballroom that President Trump has said would be built with private dollars.
item.dc:creator: Carl Hulse
item.media:credit: Doug Mills/The New York Times
item.media:description: Construction at the White House last month.

item.title: To Rename Its Airport After Trump, Palm Beach Had to License His Name
item.description: The Trumps won’t profit from airport-branded merchandise sold on site. But the agreement doesn’t prevent them from profiting off such items sold elsewhere.
item.dc:creator: Patricia Mazzei
item.media:credit: Haiyun Jiang for The New York Times
item.media:description: Originally opened in 1936 as Morrison Field, the Florida airport was renamed Palm Beach International Airport in 1948.

item.title: Judge Refers Justice Dept. Lawyer for Possible Discipline, Calling Out ‘Lack of Candor’
item.description: Officials withheld a detainee’s overseas arrest warrant from a federal judge. When she ordered his release, they used the same information to attack her publicly.
item.dc:creator: Mattathias Schwartz
item.media:credit: USA Today Network, via Reuters Connect
item.media:description: Judge Melissa R. DuBose, left, after being sworn in as a U.S. district judge in Cranston, R.I., last year.

item.title: Suspect in National Mall Shooting Expressed Hostility Toward White House, Authorities Say
item.description: Michael Marx, a 45-year-old Texan, is accused of shooting at Secret Service agents by the Washington Monument on Monday.
item.dc:creator: Minho Kim, Glenn Thrush, Jacey Fortin and Jin Yu Young
item.media:credit: Andrew Leyden/Getty Images
item.media:description: A Secret Service agent standing guard at a security perimeter on the National Mall after the shooting on Monday.

item.title: F.D.A. Blocked Publication of Research Finding Covid and Shingles Vaccines Were Safe
item.description: The agency’s scientists and data contractors reviewed millions of patient records for studies that were pulled back before release.
item.dc:creator: Christina Jewett
item.category: Centers for Disease Control and Prevention
item.media:credit: Kim Raff for The New York Times
item.media:description: A Covid-19 vaccine distribution site in Salt Lake City in 2022.

item.title: With Free Vodka, and a Few Protests, Russia Returns to the Venice Biennale
item.description: On Tuesday, Russia opened a pavilion at the Venice Biennale for the first since its full-scale invasion of Ukraine. Ukraine’s own exhibit is just a stone’s throw.
item.dc:creator: Alex Marshall
item.media:credit: Matteo de Mayda for The New York Times

item.title: Venice Biennale: In Photos and Video
item.description: Scenes from the highly anticipated art event.
item.dc:creator: Matteo de Mayda
item.media:credit: Matteo de Mayda for The New York Times

item.title: The Venice Biennale and Its Many Flashpoints: An Explainer
item.description: The sudden death of a curator, the surprise participation of Russia and an unconventional U.S. entry are heating up the art world Olympics, which begins previews Tuesday.
item.dc:creator: Zachary Small
item.category: War Crimes, Genocide and Crimes Against Humanity
item.media:credit: Casey Kelbaugh for The New York Times
item.media:description: The central pavilion of the Giardini on Tuesday, first day of previews for the 61st Venice Biennale. The exhibition, “In Minor Keys,” was initiated by Koyo Kouoh and overseen by her team after her death last year.

item.title: What Happened When Pope Leo XIV Had to Call Customer Service
item.description: Spoiler alert: There was no miracle.
item.dc:creator: Julie Bosman
item.media:credit: Alessandra Tarantino/Associated Press
item.media:description: Pope Leo XIV has a palace, the Swiss Guard and the popemobile. But he also has hassles, just like everyone else.

item.title: N.Y.P.D. Captain Is Transferred After Calling Mamdani ‘Total Nonsense’
item.description: The captain, James G. Wilson, was also filmed calling Democrats a “waste of human race.”
item.dc:creator: Maria Cramer and Andy Newman
item.media:credit: Mary Altaffer/Associated Press

item.title: Pro-Palestinian and Pro-Israel Demonstrators Rally Outside Synagogue
item.description: For the second time in six months, protesters gathered outside Park East Synagogue, which was holding an event to promote real estate sales in Israel. A heavy police presence kept the two groups apart.
item.dc:creator: Liam Stack and Nate Schweber
item.media:credit: Ryan Murphy for The New York Times
item.media:description: Protesters opposed to the event at Park East Synagogue included pro-Palestinian groups and members of an Orthodox sect.

item.title: Was It Art? Was It Fashion? Was It Good?
item.description: Guests at the Met Gala had different interpretations of the night’s dress code.
item.dc:creator: Vanessa Friedman
item.media:credit: Nina Westervelt for The New York Times
item.media:description: Kim Kardashian, in an orange fiberglass breastplate by the British pop artist Allen Jones.

item.title: How Heidi Klum Got Her 2026 Met Gala Statue Look
item.description: “I could eat and use my hands quite comfortably,” Klum said of her intricate Roman statue ensemble, which was inspired by a marble sculpture by Raffaele Monti.
item.dc:creator: Alisha Haridasani Gupta
item.media:credit: Nina Westervelt for The New York Times
item.media:description: Heidi Klum arrived at the Met Gala, which had the theme “Fashion Is Art,” dressed as a marble statue.

item.title: Apple Reaches $250 Million Settlement Over Claims It Misled People on A.I.
item.description: Some iPhone owners will be eligible to receive $25 to $95 over claims that the tech giant oversold its artificial intelligence system, Apple Intelligence.
item.dc:creator: David McCabe and Kalley Huang
item.media:credit: Lucia Vazquez for The New York Times
item.media:description: The Apple store at Grand Central Station in Manhattan.

item.title: Elon Musk Wanted OpenAI to Go Commercial, Greg Brockman Testifies
item.description: Greg Brockman, OpenAI’s president, testified in a trial pitting Mr. Musk against his company that the world’s richest man was eager to change how it operated as a nonprofit.
item.dc:creator: Cade Metz and Mike Isaac
item.media:credit: Jason Henry for The New York Times
item.media:description: OpenAI’s president, Greg Brockman, with his wife, Anna Brockman, at the federal courthouse in Oakland, Calif., on Tuesday. He testified for a second day.

item.title: ‘Avatar’ Suit Focuses on Hot Topic in A.I. Age: A Character’s Face
item.description: An actress accused the director James Cameron of stealing her likeness for a digitally created, blue-skinned warrior princess.
item.dc:creator: Brooks Barnes

item.title: Japan’s Takaichi Pitches Strength and Stability in Visits to Australia and Vietnam
item.description: Prime Minister Sanae Takaichi is attempting to raise Japan’s profile at a time when China’s clout is rising and doubts about the United States are spreading.
item.dc:creator: Javier C. Hernández
item.media:credit: Hollie Adams/Reuters
item.media:description: Prime Minister Sanae Takaichi of Japan arriving in Canberra, Australia, on Sunday.

item.title: Modi’s Triumph in West Bengal Elections Puts Him Closer to an Opposition-Free India
item.description: With his triumph in West Bengal state elections, Prime Minister Narendra Modi has moved closer to his dream of an opposition-free India.
item.dc:creator: Alex Travelli, Hari Kumar and Pragati K.B.
item.media:credit: Sahiba Chawdhary/Reuters
item.media:description: Supporters of Mr. Modi’s Bharatiya Janata Party celebrating in Kolkata as the party headed to victory in the West Bengal state assembly elections on Monday.

item.title: Deadly Russian Strikes Rip Into Ukrainian Cities
item.description: At least 12 people were killed in Zaporizhzhia, one of the highest tolls from a single attack so far this year. The cities of Dnipro and Kramatorsk were also attacked.
item.dc:creator: Cassandra Vinograd
item.media:credit: Serhii Chalyi/Reuters
item.media:description: The site of a Russian airstrike in Zaporizhzhia, Ukraine, on Tuesday. The death toll in Zaporizhzhia was one of the highest from a single attack so far this year.

item.title: Cruise Ship Struck by Hantavirus Is to Head to Canary Islands, W.H.O. Says
item.description: The Spanish government will receive the vessel. The World Health Organization said human-to-human transmission may have played a role in the outbreak.
item.dc:creator: Max Kim, Lynsey Chutel and Claire Moses
item.media:credit: Arilson Almeida/Associated Press
item.media:description: The MV Hondius moored off the coast of Cape Verde, an archipelago in the Atlantic Ocean, on Monday.

item.title: Hantavirus Outbreaks Are Rare, but They Aren’t Going Away and There’s No Cure
item.description: Since the family of rodent-borne infections were identified in the 1950s, they have turned up all over the world.
item.dc:creator: Adeel Hassan
item.media:credit: -/Agence France-Presse — Getty Images
item.media:description: A cruise ship remained stranded off the coast of Cape Verde after a suspected hantavirus outbreak on the vessel killed at least three people and left several others ill.

item.title: A Democrat Who Makes Me Listen
item.description: An interview with Representative Jake Auchincloss, a Democrat from the charismatic center.
item.dc:creator: Bret Stephens
item.media:credit: Greg Kahn for The New York Times

item.title: French Literature Is in an Uproar
item.description: Changes at a famous French publishing house tell us a lot about the world we live in now.
item.dc:creator: Olivier Guez

item.title: Late Night Cringes at Trump’s Chatter in Front of Children
item.description: “How are you talking about nuclear war in front of children?” Desi Lydic asked after the president discussed the Iran conflict in a room full of students on Tuesday.
item.dc:creator: Trish Bendix
item.category: Late Show with Stephen Colbert (TV Program)
Late Night with Seth Meyers (TV Program)
The Daily Show with Jon Stewart (TV Program)

item.title: Illinois State Police to Investigate Fatal ICE Shooting
item.description: Officials said they were examining the shooting of Silverio Villegas-Gonzalez last summer during a Chicago-area crackdown on illegal immigration.
item.dc:creator: Mitch Smith
item.media:credit: Todd Heisler/The New York Times
item.media:description: A memorial for Silverio Villegas-Gonzalez, who was shot by an ICE agent in Franklin Park, Ill., last year.

item.title: Louise Arbour Named as Canada’s Governor General
item.description: Prime Minister Mark Carney’s appointment of Louise Arbour, a former Supreme Court justice, as governor general, sends a message beyond Canada.
item.dc:creator: Ian Austen
item.category: Charles III, King of the United Kingdom
item.media:credit: Adrian Wyld/The Canadian Press, via Associated Press
item.media:description: Prime Minister Mark Carney of Canada with the country’s new governor general, Louise Arbour, in Ottawa on Tuesday.

item.title: Journalist and Her Mother Are Among 3 Killed in Upper Manhattan Fire
item.description: Yolaine Díaz, a fashion and beauty writer for People en Español, and her mother were killed in the blaze at an apartment building in Inwood, People magazine said.
item.dc:creator: Ashley Southall
item.category: Department of Housing Preservation and Development (NYC)
item.media:credit: Dakota Santiago for The New York Times
item.media:description: Yolaine Diaz, Ana Mirtha Lantigua and an unidentified third person were pronounced dead at the scene of a fire in the Inwood neighborhood of Manhattan on Monday.

item.title: A Solitary Protester on Top of the Frederick Douglass Memorial Bridge in D.C.
item.description: For five days, a 45-year-old former jeweler has camped on top of the Frederick Douglass Memorial Bridge, drawing a mix of support, disapproval and concern.
item.dc:creator: Chris Hippensteel and JoAnna Daemmrich
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:credit: Ken Cedeno/Reuters
item.media:description: Guido Reichstadter atop the Frederick Douglass Memorial Bridge in Washington, D.C. on Saturday. His protest there has gone on longer than he had planned.
